# UN Secretary-General Urges Immediate Restoration of Navigational Rights in Strait of Hormuz per Security Council Resolution 2817

## Overview
UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for the immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to restore navigational rights and freedoms, citing Security Council Resolution 2817 amid a Middle East crisis entering its third month. He emphasized the need for safe, predictable shipping to mitigate global economic impacts from the blockade. Guterres urged all parties to uphold a fragile ceasefire and pursue a comprehensive resolution.

## Details
During a press stakeout on the Middle East, Guterres expressed deep concern over the curtailment of navigational rights in the Strait of Hormuz, noting daily escalations in costs and global economic reverberations.[1] He directly invoked **Security Council Resolution 2817**, demanding its implementation to open the Strait and allow all ships to pass freely.[1] Guterres highlighted ongoing contacts with parties via his Personal Envoy Jean Arnault, with all interlocutors agreeing on the need for a peaceful, durable resolution despite differing views.[1] He also addressed peacekeeping funding, affirming assessed contributions as non-negotiable obligations of Member States while pursuing UN80 reforms for efficiency.[1] In the context of a fragile ceasefire, humanitarian corridors stayed mentioned as a worst-case contingency, but the primary goal remains total freedom of navigation.[1]

## Location
New York, United States

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @UN (United Nations). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
